Enron Corp. Announces $1.2 Billion Credit Line

04/04/2001
Reuters English News Service
(C) Reuters Limited 2001.

HOUSTON, April 4 (Reuters) - Enron Corp., the world's largest energy trader, said it had
raised $1.2 billion in a credit line with Citigroup Inc.

The company said it had also secured $1.1 billion in additional credit lines from
other lenders.

Enron said it would use the credit lines to fund its operations and to repay
its existing debt.

The company said it would use the credit lines to repay its existing
debt, which stood at $10.5 billion at the end of March.

11/04/2001
Reuters English News Service
(C) Reuters Limited 2001.

HOUSTON, Nov 4 (Reuters) - Enron Corp., the world's largest energy trader, said it had secured $1.2 billion in credit from a group of lenders led by Citigroup Inc.

The company said it had also secured $1.1 billion in credit lines from other lenders, including Bank of America Corp., Citigroup, Deutsche Bank AG, J.P. Morgan Chase & Co. and Morgan Stanley Dean Witter.

Enron said it would use the credit lines to repay its $1.1 billion revolving credit facility with Citigroup and to repay its $1.2 billion revolving credit facility with J.P. Morgan Chase & Co.
